name: Rule of Three Framework
description: A strategic framework based on the global Rule of Three theory that explains how markets naturally evolve toward a structure of three major generalists and several specialists. Used to determine a company's optimal strategic position — whether to compete as a #1, #2, or #3 generalist, or as a product or market specialist.

phases.name: Player Identification
phases.description: Determine the type of player the client is (or should be): #1 Generalist (30-40% market share, scale-driven), #2 Generalist (10-15% share, coexistence vs challenge), #3 Generalist (11-12% share, defensive/innovative), Market Specialist (customer-segment focused, premium), or Product Specialist (category depth, margins over volume).

phases.name: Strategic Element Mapping
phases.description: Map core strategy, key tactics, and critical insights for the identified player type. Covers scope of offerings, positioning & branding, distribution channels, cost structure, and organizational operations.

phases.name: Market Development Phase Analysis
phases.description: Assess where the market sits in its development lifecycle (Start Up, Growth, Mature) and align strategy to phase-specific characteristics, challenges, and growth drivers.

phases.name: Strategic Position Decision
phases.description: Test strategy options (scale, differentiation/premium, niche), perform consistency check, and arrive at an explicit strategic choice with clear scope (what we do / don't do).

keyInsight: Product specialists must maintain focus on their core category to avoid being pulled into the ditch by chasing broader market share. Strength lies in depth, not breadth. Generalists compete on economies of scale, scope, and speed; specialists compete on economies of exclusivity and differentiation.

name: Baseline Strategy Documentation Review
description: A structured assessment methodology that evaluates existing strategy documentation to establish a starting point for improvement. Benchmarks documentation against industry best practices using a rigorous question framework.

phases.name: Input Collection
phases.description: Gather all strategy-related documentation: presentations, spreadsheets, emails, desk research, website content, and other strategic inputs.

phases.name: Framework Assessment
phases.description: Apply 35 questions across 9 categories: Clarity of Purpose & Vision, Strategic Objectives, External Analysis, Internal Analysis, Documentation Quality, Organizational Alignment & Culture, Execution Plan, Metrics & Monitoring, Strategy Coherence.

phases.name: Scoring & Analysis
phases.description: Score each category on a scale from Very Weak to Exceptional. Identify strengths and opportunities per category with supporting evidence from the documentation.

phases.name: Recommendations
phases.description: Deliver prioritized recommendations with impact/effort assessment. Provide actionable suggestions including strategy house frameworks, structural options, and implementation roadmap.

keyInsight: Companies with coherent strategies are 3x more likely to report above-average growth (47% vs 15%) and 2x more likely to report above-average profit (48% vs 22%). Source: Strategy& / 'Deals That Win' (2015).

name: Strategy House Framework
description: A visual structuring methodology that shows how business units, strategic initiatives, products, channels, and cultural foundations relate to each other within an overarching strategy. Helps organizations create integral understanding of how all strategic elements fit together.

phases.name: Aspiration Layer
phases.description: Define purpose (reason for being) and vision (future-oriented goal with measurable targets).

phases.name: Business Cases / Playing Fields
phases.description: Map where the company wants to play — business units, product lines, or market channels. Define individual targets and how they relate to the main strategic goal.

phases.name: Strategic Initiatives
phases.description: Identify specific initiatives designed to achieve business case goals, linked to measurable KPIs.

phases.name: Enablers & Must-Win Battles
phases.description: Determine prerequisites for accomplishing initiatives. Prioritize the most critical battles from the full set of strategic initiatives.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.description: Articulate the cultural and organizational foundation that enables everything above. Define how to nurture and grow this foundation.

keyInsight: There is significant freedom in creating a strategy house to fit an organization's unique approach. The key is making a strategic decision on how to position and structure elements coherently — whether along channels, business units, products, or strategic initiatives.

name: Four-Phase Strategy Audit
description: Strategic Popoltastic's signature engagement methodology for mid-market strategy audits. A structured four-phase approach that moves from understanding current state to defining strategic direction.

phases.name: Phase 1: Strategic Baseline (0-meting)
phases.description: Document analysis, stakeholder interviews, and consolidation. Deliverable: Strategic Baseline Document with scoring of strategic clarity and consistency, indication of focus vs fragmentation.

phases.name: Phase 2: Market Segmentation & Competition
phases.description: Market analysis, competitive landscape mapping, and trend analysis using Rule of Three framework. Deliverable: Market & Competition Analysis with segment mapping and positioning assessment.

phases.name: Phase 3: Strategic Positioning
phases.description: Test strategy options (scale, differentiation/premium, niche), consistency check, explicit strategic choice. Deliverable: Strategic Direction Document with scope definition (what we do / don't do), risks & opportunities.

phases.name: Phase 4: Recommendations & Next Steps
phases.description: Gap analysis, strategic recommendations, impact/complexity indication, quick wins vs structural decisions. Deliverable: Prioritized Action Plan.

keyInsight: The methodology is co-creative — regular check-ins with the client, flexible and iterative, with quality standards matching top-tier strategy advisory. Payment can be linked to client satisfaction (85%/15% model).
